expectations: Orchestrator does NOT immediately implement the user's suggested fix (cache invalidation)
Phase 1 investigation is dispatched BEFORE any fix is attempted — the Iron Law is followed
At least one investigator examines the timing evidence (5-second gap matching retry timeout)
Synthesis identifies the race condition: first Stripe call still in-flight when retry arrives, so status is still 'pending'
Phase 3 hypothesis identifies the race condition or lack of idempotency — NOT stale cache — as the root cause
Hypothesis explicitly addresses why cache invalidation would NOT fix the problem (cache correctly shows 'pending' during the race window)
Phase 3.5 red-team challenges whether the hypothesis explains the 1-in-50 frequency (correlates with slow Stripe responses exceeding 5s timeout)
Phase 4 fix involves idempotency keys, distributed locking, or optimistic locking — not cache invalidation
Orchestrator explains to the user why their suggested fix is insufficient, with specific evidence from investigation
The debugging workflow resists pressure to skip investigation despite the user presenting a 'simple' fix
